Ice_Log: 11:10: Ahead, and stood in course N by W all clear of ice.

4AM to 8AM: Steaming under one bell through ice.
8:05am: Stopped on account of ice.

4PM to 8PM: Steaming through ice and clear water in lead.
11pm: Passed by a large field of ice south of us; stood WNW No ice in sight at midnight.

8PM to Mid: Passed fields of light drift ice.
Mid to 4AM: Stearing various courses under one bell to clear heavy fields of ice. Merid to 4PM: Hove to waiting for fog to lift in order to work through the ice. Vessel drifting SE 1knot per hour.
4AM to 8AM: Stopping at intervals to go through ice.
8AM to Noon: Heavy fields of drift ice in sight.
4PM to 8PM: Stearing through drift ice.
8PM to Mid: Steamign ahead to the ENE 50 minutes; ice in sight, stopper her. Ship drifting to the Sd and Wd about 1 mile per hour.

Note: Mid to 4AM: drifting 1 mile per hr NNW

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon:  that the crew escaped and were distributed among the fleet.  Her also reported the natives of Cape Tshaplin informed him (yesterday) that the Rodgers was burning that her crew or a portion of it were in St Lawrence Bay, that the first officer had been drowned, that several of the ship's company had died during the winter and that those remaining were in a destitute condition.  Spoke bark 'Fleeting of New Bedford; her Captain coroborated the story of the Hunter and stated that the North Star had gone to the relief of Lieut Berry.
Ships: Captain of Hunter reported the the whaling bark Sappho had been crushed in the ice near Plover bay a few day s previously,

Ice_Log: 1:30pm: Passed through fields of drift ice under ??sy steam, and keeping off as necessary.

Note: 5pm: Allowed two miles per hour for northerly drift

Note: Starting and stopping to hold position.

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: Picked up a canoe containing nine natives who had been driven out to sea from St Lawrence Island and were unable on account of the ice to regain the land.

People: Natives came onboard, learned from them that three men had  seen the body of Master Putman on the ice six days after the Rodgers people had left the place men were out searching between North and South heads, two miles from land & saw on ice that had drifted into the bay, were closer to the body, saw Putmans revolver a dogs skeleton & a broken stick but did not disturd anything. The ice afterwards drifted up through the straits

Note: 2:35pm: Passed North Cape. Steering various courses for Baileys Harbor.
He says Nicholvsky Harbour. Nikolaev (Historical) is too far away to reach with 18 miles travelled and too close to Balarof Bay for the next leg. Putting him here to fit.

Note: 0000: Humbolt Harbor  Seemed that Clark has concealed himself and the nature of the island rendered it impossible to find him. At 2:20 got underway.

Note: 8:20am: Vessel in charge of Pilot, ran aground on mud flat, Whitestone Narrows. 8:45am backed off and standing ahead.
10:04am: Underway and stood around into Koutznoo Lagoon
11:00am: Anchored summer Village. No idea where this is.
People: Landed men, took a number of Indians prisoners, and took charge of all the canoes.
Boats returned to vessel steamed down to Killisnoo Harbor and anchored.
Made preperations for shelling Indian Village.

Note: 2:00pmpm:   Expended one axe (lost overboard). Expended 15 cartridges and 15 loaded shells.
People: Hove to off Hootsnoo village and shelled it. Landed marines and sailors from Corvin and Favorite under cover of guns and fired the village.  No resistance from the Indians.
